topic: WIN-067 detail_extra defense-neutralization block (post-EXP-203) — Antarctic gravity hole, c5 review of 3-point c4 advocate rebuttal closure
change_summary: EXP-203 integrated into WIN-067.detail_extra (previously null): five-paragraph defense-neutralization block closing the c4 advocate's three-point rebuttal. Opener + (i) terminology pivot from V51.0 'toroidal sump node' to V51.2 'Sub-terrestrial structure' + (ii) 170×→1,430× Antarctic-latitude recomputation + (iii) Domain 4/Domain 19 verbatim anchors + 'Net effect' closer. detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text unchanged by this EXP.
our_argument_summary: The new detail_extra preemptively closes the c4 advocate's three-point rebuttal (terminology, Domain-numbering parenthetical, 170× headline inflation) by (1) conceding the label change is immaterial because no mechanism equation is attached to either label; (2) computing the apples-to-apples Antarctic-latitude gravity ratio at ≈1,430× using the advocate's own cited figure 9.826 m/s² anchored to WGS84 polar 9.8322 m/s²; (3) correcting Domain #12 → Domain 4 with verbatim quotes of the Domain 4 formula and its falsification criterion, then anchoring the one-sided Domain 19 'Anomaly <20 mGal' threshold as pass-by-threshold-not-prediction. The closer binds the dome author to his own 'no exceptions' falsifiability statement.

relevant_files: data/wins.json WIN-067 (detail_extra, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, finding)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome publishes two gravity descriptions: (a) the H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) column-height function (sections.json, walkthrough Section 1.1), which under any monotone coupling predicts 38–90% gravity drop at r=20,015 km; (b) a Domain 4 closed-form g(r) = 9.7803·(1 + 0.005307·exp(−r/λg)), which evaluates to WGS84 pole value 9.8322 at r=0 and 9.7854 at r=20,015 — the latter OVERSHOOTS Domain 4's own 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' falsification by 4.8×, because real polar g is 9.8322. The detail_extra block targets (a) rhetorically and quotes (b) verbatim but does not evaluate (b) at the rim.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 4 formula evaluated in monitor.py at r=0 only (per detail_evidence); live-power page displays 9.8322 and labels it as matching WGS84. H(r) is declared but never back-computed to gravity in monitor.py. No live fetch of Antarctic gravity data.
data_sources_used: live-power page (john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html) — Domain 4 and Domain 19 verbatim
Ferraccioli et al. 2011 Nature (East Antarctic mantle plume)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 19 'Anomaly <20 mGal' is a one-sided threshold (falsified below the floor, auto-passes above). The 'Sub-terrestrial structure' correlation line has no mechanism field. Domain 4 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' is a reference-check against the globe model — the dome concedes at its own falsifiability point that its gravity formula must match WGS84.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The detail_evidence text still carries a residual version tag 'monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1' while the new detail_extra cites V51.2 live-power-page Domain 4 throughout. The EXP-203 notes explicitly flag this line as a deferred decider-level patch. Readers see both V51.1 and V51.2 in the same WIN.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py evaluates Domain 4 at r=0 only, which yields the WGS84-tautology value; no external fetch of Antarctic gravity or Mascon anomaly occurs in the repo. The 'monitoring: none' tag is inherited correctly from prior cycles — no fetch-based test of the Antarctic claim exists.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics explains the East Antarctic Bouguer anomaly via an isostatically-uncompensated mantle plume plus post-glacial isostatic adjustment (Ferraccioli 2011, Shepherd 2012, Pappa 2019). The dome renames the feature 'Sub-terrestrial structure' (V51.2, Domain 19) or — in snapshots predating the V51.2 edit — 'toroidal sump node,' without specifying a formula linking dome geometry to the observed magnitude or azimuthal asymmetry. Pure relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: GRACE/GRACE-FO Level-2 gravity products span 2002–present and the Pappa 2019 Bouguer compilation was published years before the dome's V51 series. The dome monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line (per prior-cycle detail_verdict_text), so temporal-priority from the repo history cannot be checked.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The Domain 4 closed-form g(r) is WGS84 constants wearing an exponential decoration that collapses at the one r (=0) the monitor tests. The H(r) mechanism is asserted but never solved for g. The 'Sub-terrestrial structure' correlation line has no mechanism field. No dome-geometric derivation path leads to a specific value of g at any r ≠ 0.

holes_found.description: Unit conversion error in paragraph (ii). The new text reads 'Recomputing against the actual Mascon-Bouguer anomaly (-40 to -60 mGal ≈ 5×10⁻⁵ m/s²)…widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude.' The conversion is off by a factor of 10: 1 mGal = 10⁻⁵ m/s², so -40 to -60 mGal ≈ -4 to -6 ×10⁻⁴ m/s² (midpoint 5×10⁻⁴), not 5×10⁻⁵. With the correct denominator 5×10⁻⁴ m/s², the ratio becomes 8.868 / 5×10⁻⁴ ≈ 17,700× — one order of magnitude worse than 1,430×, not two. The text consequently overstates the Mascon-referenced miss by another 10× and makes a numerical claim a careful dome defender can disprove with a two-line unit check. A dome defender reading the block will go: 'you said 50 mGal = 5×10⁻⁵ m/s², that is 5 mGal; your arithmetic is wrong by ten. Your 1,430× ratio may survive but the 143,000× number does not.' This is a quality-control failure in a paragraph whose entire rhetorical purpose is demonstrating that the honest correction deepens the critique.
holes_found.recommendation: Two single-change edits: (1) replace '≈ 5×10⁻⁵ m/s²' with '≈ 5×10⁻⁴ m/s²' in the parenthetical; (2) replace 'widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude' with 'widens the miss by another order of magnitude' (and, if the exact number is desired, '≈ 17,700×'). Net effect on the argument is minor — the miss against the Mascon anomaly is still ~12× worse than the 1,430× Antarctic-latitude ratio — but the text becomes defensible.

holes_found.description: The opener and paragraphs (i)–(iii) reference 'the c4 advocate,' 'the advocate's own cited value,' 'the advocate is correct,' and link to 'monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c4.json' with 'defense_survives=4' metadata. This is internal pipeline jargon. A mainstream reader of funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review has no visibility into the curmudgeon/analyst loop, does not know what 'c4' means, and will read 'the advocate' as either the dome author's advocate or an unnamed third party. The GitHub-link to WIN-067.c4.json exposes raw review artifacts. Even the dome author (who may recognize some of the labels) will not have context for 'defense_survives=4'. This self-referential style contrasts sharply with the rest of the review, which reads as scientific prose — the new block reads as internal process documentation promoted to public text.
holes_found.recommendation: De-jargon the opener and maintain scientific voice. Replace 'The curmudgeon's c4 advocate pass (WIN-067.c4.json, defense_survives=4) raises three lines of defense' with something like 'A dome defender facing our original critique has three lines of retort: (1) the phrase \'toroidal sump node\' is not in the current V51.2 page; (2) the Domain #12 parenthetical is miscited; (3) the 170× ratio is inflated by a whole-globe reference. Each folds under the dome's own live-power page.' Delete the GitHub link to the .c4.json review file. In paragraphs (ii) and (iii), replace 'the advocate' with 'a dome defender' or 'the objection' throughout. The rhetorical shape survives; only the meta-jargon changes.

holes_found.description: Headline-number inconsistency between the WIN's top-level fields and the new detail_extra. The finding reads '170× error,' tldr_evidence says 'a discrepancy of 170× with the dome's predicted variation,' tldr_verdict says 'off by a factor of 170,' and detail_verdict_text computes 'ratio: 90%/0.53% ≈ 170×.' The new paragraph (ii) asserts that when compared apples-to-apples at Antarctic latitude, the ratio is '≈ 1,430×, almost an order of magnitude worse than the 170× headline' — confirming the headline is UNDER-inflated, not OVER-inflated as the advocate suggested. A thoughtful reader sees the 170× headline four times at the top of the panel, then reads '1,430×' in the closing paragraph, and is left to reconcile the two numbers. A dome defender will say: 'you've conceded in your own closing block that 1,430× is the more rigorous number — why does your finding still say 170×? Which is THE headline?' This is unforced inconsistency: the moment the curmudgeon accepted the analyst's 1,430× recomputation, the top-level fields should have been updated in lockstep, or the new block should have named 170× as the \'generous / whole-globe comparison\' headline explicitly.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one: (a) Update finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, and detail_verdict_text to cite 1,430× as the headline and footnote 170× as the whole-globe-spread reference; (b) Keep 170× as the headline and revise paragraph (ii)'s closer to 'the apples-to-apples ratio at Antarctic latitude is ~1,430×, strengthening the headline 170× rather than retiring it.' Option (a) is the stronger analytical move; option (b) preserves published fingerprint stability. Either way the headline and closing block must reconcile without leaving a gap the defender steps into.

holes_found.description: The ~9.826 m/s² Antarctic-latitude gravity figure used in paragraph (ii)'s anchor arithmetic has no primary-source citation — it is attributed to 'the advocate's own cited value.' That attribution is internal (the advocate was our adversarial review pass) and the figure is approximate. A rigorous defender will say 'pin your number to a primary source.' Antarctic-interior g varies substantially with latitude and elevation: at the South Pole Station (2,835 m elevation) the free-air-corrected measured g is ~9.808 m/s²; at sea-level-equivalent the inferred g at the pole is ~9.8322 m/s². The figure 9.826 is a reasonable mean for Antarctic latitudes 70–85°S at surface elevations, but without a citation the whole 1,430× arithmetic is hanging on an unsourced number.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a primary-source citation to the 9.826 m/s² figure. Options: IAG's International Gravity Formula 1980 evaluated at 80°S (≈9.831 m/s² sea level, ≈9.809 at 2,500 m), or the Antarctic Geoid Project (AGP) gravity database, or a specific South-Pole-Station reference. The anticipated objection in EXP-203 (response to 'advocate's off-the-cuff number') notes that different values only make the ratio worse — bake that note into the prose as a single sentence: 'If a defender rejects 9.826 m/s² in favor of the WGS84 polar value 9.8322 m/s², the observed drop goes to zero and the ratio diverges; the critique strengthens under either choice.'

holes_found.description: Residual V51.0 / V51.1 version references in detail_evidence coexist with the new block's V51.2 citations. detail_evidence contains: 'no such mechanism appears in V51.0' and '(monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1).' The new detail_extra cites V51.2 throughout. The same WIN panel thus cites three different dome versions without a note about the jump. A defender can say: 'you're mixing V51.0, V51.1, and V51.2 references. Are you reviewing my current page or an archive?' The EXP-203 notes explicitly flag the V51.1 line as deferred to decider-level patch, but the patch hasn't landed and the review now sits with pieces pointing at different versions.
holes_found.recommendation: Single-line update to detail_evidence: replace '(monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1)' with '(V51.2 live-power page, Domain 4 equatorial gravity entry; identical formula in V51.1 monitor.py).' This preserves the historical-version continuity claim while anchoring the current citation to V51.2. The 'no such mechanism appears in V51.0' reference can stay — it is making a point about the older snapshot.

holes_found.description: Paragraph (iii) calls the Domain 4 exponential 'decorative': 'a decorative exponential that collapses at r = 0 (exp(0) = 1).' The exponential exp(−r/8,619) is not decorative — it varies from 1 at r=0 to 0.098 at r=20,015 km, spanning an order of magnitude. Across that range the Domain 4 formula predicts g going from 9.8322 (WGS84 pole) at r=0 to 9.7854 at r=20,015 (near-equatorial value). This REPRODUCES the WGS84 pole-to-equator flattening curve at the wrong locations — it says g is maximum at dome-center and drops toward the rim. A dome defender will point this out: 'your own words admit you haven't evaluated my formula anywhere but r=0. The exponential is what DOES the pole-to-equator work; calling it decorative reveals you didn't read it.' Worse, the stronger argument is sitting in plain view: evaluating Domain 4 at r=20,015 km yields g=9.7854 m/s², but the REAL value at the corresponding geographic point (South Pole) is 9.8322 m/s² — a 0.476% error, which VIOLATES Domain 4's own falsification criterion ('Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard') by ~5×. The new block anchors on verbatim quotes of that criterion but then steps past the point where the dome's own formula fails it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'a decorative exponential that collapses at r = 0 (exp(0) = 1)' with a direct evaluation of Domain 4 at the rim: 'an exponential that collapses to 1 at r=0 (where monitor.py checks it, producing the WGS84-pole tautology). Evaluated at r=20,015 km — the only other geometrically-motivated point, the dome's stated rim — Domain 4 returns g = 9.7803·(1 + 0.005307·0.098) = 9.7854 m/s². The measured South-Pole gravity is 9.8322 m/s². The error is 0.476%, which VIOLATES Domain 4's own falsification criterion (Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard) by 4.8× — the formula fails its own test at the one evaluation point where it could discriminate from the globe.' This is stronger than 'decorative' and uses the dome's own rule against its own formula.

holes_found.description: The 'Net effect' paragraph posits a false dichotomy: the defense either concedes OR the dome author must 'publicly strike the Domain 4 falsification text…strike the Domain 19 one-sided threshold…and publish a mechanism equation.' A careful defender has a third option: reinterpret each domain as a limited-scope reference check. He will say: 'Domain 4 is a zeroth-order match check at r=0; any gravity model must match WGS84 there. Domain 19 Anomaly <20 mGal is a noise-floor check; a model shouldn't confuse millidal noise with a real signal. Neither falsification criterion is intended to carry the weight of a cosmological prediction — the H(r) mechanism is the dome-specific claim, and the advocate never contested that the H(r) prediction fails at the rim. You are over-reading limited-scope falsification texts as if they were model-discriminating tests.' The Net-effect paragraph doesn't preempt this retreat. The dome author can reinterpret the Domain-4-and-Domain-19 failures as tolerable while maintaining the H(r) argument — at which point the defense survives on rescope rather than retraction.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence at the end of the Net effect paragraph: 'A defender may retreat by reframing Domains 4 and 19 as limited-scope reference checks rather than model-discriminating tests; that retreat concedes the substantive point — neither closed-form formula constitutes a dome-specific forecast — and leaves the H(r) mechanism, which predicts 90% gravity drop at the rim, as the sole remaining dome prediction, unrescued.' This closes the rescope escape by naming it and pointing out it forfeits the very ground the defender is trying to hold.

holes_found.description: Paragraph (ii) arithmetic 'g ≈ 9.8322 × (837/8,537) = 0.964 m/s²' rounds 0.9640 to 0.964. Then '8.868 m/s² reduction.' The reduction 9.8322 − 0.964 = 8.8682, which rounds to 8.868. Later '0.006 m/s² below the polar reference.' The actual value is 0.0062, which rounds to 0.006. Then 'Dividing predicted drop by observed drop gives ≈ 1,430×.' Recomputing with unrounded values: 8.8682 / 0.0062 ≈ 1,430.4 → 1,430×. Consistent. But if a defender recomputes using the rounded-down 0.006 (as the text advertises), he gets 8.868 / 0.006 = 1,478 — not 1,430×. A fastidious defender may flag '1,430 does not follow from the rounded numbers as published.' Low stakes but worth a sentence clarifying the ratio is computed with unrounded values, or publishing more digits.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) publish more digits: 'predicts g ≈ 0.9640 m/s² — an 8.8682 m/s² reduction… observed drop ~0.0062 m/s² (0.063%)… ratio ≈ 1,430×'; or (b) add a parenthetical: '(ratio computed on unrounded values; rounding the drop to 0.006 gives ≈ 1,478×).' Either closes a nitpick a careful reader would reach for.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c4: HOLE-067-C4-006 (Pappa et al. 2019 / DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 not independently verified) and HOLE-067-C4-007 (tldr_evidence phrasing if any 'southern stations' imprecision remains) are not addressed by EXP-203 (the EXP explicitly notes these are deferred). They remain open. c4 review flagged them as minor; they remain minor, but the decider should either close or re-assign.
holes_found.recommendation: Keep both open; decider can schedule a follow-up EXP or bundle into a tldr/citation polishing pass across WIN-067's lesser fields.

stronger_arguments.description: Domain 4 fails its OWN falsification criterion at r=20,015 km. Evaluated explicitly: g(r=20,015) = 9.7803·(1 + 0.005307·exp(−20,015/8,619)) = 9.7803·(1 + 0.005307·0.098) = 9.7854 m/s². Real South-Pole gravity ≈ 9.8322 m/s². Error = 0.476%, which VIOLATES Domain 4's stated falsification threshold 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' by 4.8×. This is cleaner than 'the exponential is decorative at r=0': instead of arguing the formula collapses to tautology at the evaluation point, you show that at the ONLY OTHER geometrically-principled evaluation point, the formula fails by the dome author's own rule. Compounds the existing Net-effect rhetoric: the dome author need not 'publicly strike' the falsification text — the text already falsifies his own formula when evaluated where he claims the dome differs from the globe.

The OPEN-001 admission on the dome's evolution page ('Dome-native coordinates without WGS84' listed as IN PROGRESS) is the strongest anchor for 'globe-parasitic by construction.' The dome author has self-admitted that the current model cannot compute its own per-station predictions without WGS84 coordinates. EXP-205 (WIN-025 globe-fingerprint, SEC-8b.3) uses this same admission. The WIN-067 block does not cite it, even though it is directly on point for the Domain 4 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' citation — the dome's own formula can only be evaluated in globe coordinates, which is why it matches WGS84 at r=0. Citing OPEN-001 makes the tautology argument self-confirming without requiring the reader to trust our derivation.

Cross-reference to WIN-025's globe-fingerprint audit would strengthen the systemic claim. WIN-025 documents THREE distinct dome-authored formulas that all trace to WGS84/globe-measured constants (monitor.py KAPPA-squared −29 nT for eclipse, inject_ai_layer.py latitude-factor 0.81, and the per-station FSF table requiring WGS84 r values). WIN-067 shows Domain 4 is globe-parasitic; WIN-025 shows this is a systemic pattern. Paragraph (iii) could close with: 'See also WIN-025's three-formula audit: Domain 4 is not a one-off — the same globe-parasitism appears across the dome's gravity, eclipse, and geomagnetic predictions.' One sentence, substantial payoff.

kernel_of_truth.description: The defense-neutralization block's structural spine is sound. Opening with the three advocate defenses, answering each directly, and closing with a net-effect paragraph is correct rhetoric. Paragraph (i)'s pivot from V51.0 'toroidal sump node' to V51.2 'Sub-terrestrial structure' is the right move and is properly anchored to line 152 of the live-power dump; the 'critique does not depend on the label' pivot is correct and defuses a real QC failure in the c3 text. Paragraph (ii)'s 1,430× recomputation is a genuine analytical improvement over the 170× whole-globe comparison and is arithmetically correct (excluding the downstream mGal unit error, which does not contaminate the 1,430× itself). Paragraph (iii)'s verbatim-quote strategy for Domain 4 and Domain 19 is strong — the dome author cannot deny text he wrote himself on his own live-power page. The closing 'no exceptions' hook uses the author's own falsifiability standard against him.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three of the seven flagged issues are quality-control failures rather than analytical flaws: the mGal unit conversion (critical), the 'advocate' meta-jargon (major), and the 170× vs 1,430× headline mismatch (major). These add up to a block that is not polished for publication — it reads like an internal review document promoted verbatim to public prose. The 'decorative exponential' wording (moderate) and the Net-effect false dichotomy (moderate) are rhetorical overreaches that a careful defender will exploit. None of these individually sink the critique, but together they give a competent dome author several single-sentence counter-replies. The underlying analytical claim (H(r) implies 90% gravity drop at rim; observed is 0.5%; dome has no mechanism to reconcile) survives intact. What the block needs is QC polish and a swap to the stronger Domain-4-fails-its-own-rule argument, which is sitting unused in the arithmetic the block already commits to.

deeper_analysis: This is the third consecutive cycle where the c3/c4/c5 reviews have held 'current_verdict_holds: true' while finding escalating text-level quality issues. The underlying Self-Contradicted verdict is secure — the dome has no mechanism reconciling H(r)'s 10× variation with reality's 0.5% variation, and the review's core argument is airtight. What has degraded across cycles is the text-level polish: stale terminology, wrong domain numbers, mixed version references, and now unit conversion errors. The EXP-203 block is the first substantive response to c4's major holes and it genuinely closes the three advocate rebuttals; however, it introduces a critical unit error (mGal conversion) that a defender will catch, exposes internal pipeline jargon to public readers, and fails to reconcile the 170×/1,430× numbers across the WIN's fields. The analytical bones are right; the QC is behind. The strongest available argument — Domain 4 fails its own falsification criterion at the rim, by ~5× — is sitting one arithmetic step away in the block's own logic but is not made. One follow-up EXP polishing the unit conversion, de-jargonizing 'the advocate' references, reconciling the headline numbers, and adding the Domain-4-fails-own-rule arithmetic would move this WIN from c5 to publication-ready. If the dome author reads funwithscience-org.github.io carefully (he is known to), he WILL find the mGal error; we should fix it before he does.

citations_verified: Domain 4 falsification 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' — raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71 ✓
Domain 12 = Polaris Excess (NOT gravity) — raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 106 ✓
Domain 19 falsification 'Anomaly <20 mGal' — raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 128 ✓ (EXP-203 cites 'lines 127-128' which covers both header and falsification)
Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92 ('Sub-terrestrial structure') — raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152 ✓
HOW THIS WORKS: 'If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions' — raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 165 ✓
Arithmetic 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307) = 9.83220 m/s² matching WGS84 pole value ✓
Ferraccioli et al. 2011 DOI 10.1038/nature09659 — format valid (resolution not performed this cycle)
citations_failed: '-40 to -60 mGal ≈ 5×10⁻⁵ m/s²' — UNIT CONVERSION ERROR. Correct: 50 mGal = 5×10⁻⁴ m/s² (1 mGal = 10⁻⁵ m/s²). Downstream claim 'widens the miss by another two orders of magnitude' also wrong — should be 'by another order of magnitude' to ~17,700× (see critical hole 1).
9.826 m/s² Antarctic-latitude gravity figure — no primary-source citation in the block. Attributed to 'the advocate's own cited value' which is internal reference only.
citations_unchecked: Pappa et al. 2019 JGR DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 (HOLE-067-C4-006 carry-forward, not re-checked this cycle)
Shepherd et al. 2012 Science DOI 10.1126/science.1228102 (prior-cycle verified; not re-checked)
Theoretical_gravity#WGS_84 Wikipedia anchor (used in paragraph iii) — anchor name stable but not re-fetched

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am the dome author. Two responses to your c5 pass: (1) Your own closing paragraph contains a factor-of-10 unit conversion error. You write '-40 to -60 mGal ≈ 5×10⁻⁵ m/s²' — that is 5 mGal, not 50. Your downstream claim that the Mascon comparison widens the miss by two orders of magnitude reduces to one order of magnitude when the arithmetic is correct. This is exactly the kind of QC failure you accuse me of. (2) Your 'the advocate is correct that…' paragraphs presume a reader familiar with your internal curmudgeon pipeline. I have been told by at least one fair-auditor reader that they could not follow what 'the advocate' referred to; they assumed it was either my advocate or a strawman third party. Publish in scientific voice or don't publish. Separately: you cite my Domain 4 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' as if it binds me universally. It is a reference-check at r=0. If you believe Domain 4 fails at r=20,015, do the arithmetic and show me — you didn't. (As a bonus aside: calling the exponential 'decorative' is not correct. It is what produces the pole-to-equator flattening in the formula — collapsing it to WGS84 tautology would require setting k=0, not setting r=0.)
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three patches to close this c5 defense before the dome author reads it: (1) Fix the mGal unit conversion (5×10⁻⁴ m/s², one order of magnitude) — this is a must-fix. (2) De-jargon the 'advocate' references throughout the block — replace with 'a dome defender' or 'the objection' — and delete the GitHub link to the .c4.json file. (3) Add the Domain-4-fails-own-rule arithmetic: at r=20,015, g=9.7854, error 0.476%, violates '>0.1%' rule by 4.8×. This single addition converts the 'decorative exponential' paragraph from a rhetorical overreach into a dome-own-rule-violation, strengthening the argument while closing the defender's best remaining line of retreat. Optional: reconcile the 170×/1,430× headline mismatch (explicit footnote in finding/tldrs) and add a primary-source citation for 9.826 m/s².

cross_win_consistency.description: WIN-067 defense-neutralization invokes several cross-WIN claims that should be checked against their canonical locations.

checks.claim: WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble) — not directly invoked in WIN-067 detail_extra but relevant to the systemic globe-parasitism pattern. No inconsistency.

WIN-025 (globe-fingerprint) — not invoked in detail_extra. MISSED OPPORTUNITY: WIN-025 documents the same globe-parasitism pattern across three formulas. Adding a cross-link would strengthen the systemic critique (see stronger_arguments #3).

Part 2 / Part 3.1b reference in detail_verdict_text links to '#posthoc-standard' anchor — V6 renumbering map does not explicitly map 'Part 3.1b'. Likely pre-existing carry-forward; not introduced by EXP-203. Flag for integrity agent.

Domain 4 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' — verbatim match to raw-text line 71. Domain 19 'Anomaly <20 mGal' — verbatim match to raw-text line 128. Sub-terrestrial structure label — verbatim match to line 152. All three cross-references are dome-primary-source accurate.

EXP-203 anticipated_objections contain responses to defender objections (advocate's off-the-cuff 9.826, Domain 4 shorthand, Domain 19 floor check). These responses are NOT baked into the rendered text — they live only in the EXP JSON. The published block does not preempt two of them (the 9.826 off-the-cuff defense and the 'reference-check only' retreat). See moderate hole on Net effect false dichotomy.

checks_performed.verdict: match (rounding); see minor hole on rounding-vs-ratio consistency

checks_performed.claim: Mascon-referenced miss widens by two orders of magnitude
checks_performed.computed: 8.868 / 5×10⁻⁴ = 17,736× → 12.4× worse than 1,430× → ONE order of magnitude
checks_performed.verdict: FAIL — one order of magnitude is the correct claim

quantitative_verification.summary: Two quantitative failures, both in the mGal-referenced sentence of paragraph (ii). The 1,430× Antarctic-latitude ratio itself is correct; the Mascon-anomaly order-of-magnitude claim is wrong. The stronger argument — Domain 4 fails its own falsification criterion at r=20,015 by 4.8× — is derivable from the block's own quoted formula but is not made in the text.

tldr_review.tldr_evidence_check: 'The dome's own height formula predicts gravity drops to ~10% of normal at the Antarctic rim. Real measurements show gravity varies by only 0.5% across the whole globe (9.780 m/s² at the equator to 9.832 m/s² at the pole) — a discrepancy of 170× with the dome's predicted variation.' Factually correct but stale after EXP-203: the new block establishes 1,430× as the apples-to-apples figure. TLDR still cites 170×. Per major hole 3, this is unreconciled.
tldr_review.tldr_verdict_check: 'The dome's own formula predicts 90% gravity loss at the rim; actual variation is 0.5% — off by a factor of 170. The \'Sub-terrestrial structure\' escape hatch has no equations and no measurements.' Consistent with detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. Same 170× staleness as above — see major hole 3.
tldr_review.severity_note: Both TLDRs are factually correct at their current 170× figure; the issue is that EXP-203 establishes a sharper number in detail_extra and the TLDRs were not updated in lockstep. Flag as major because readers see TLDRs first and the mismatch is directly visible.
